volumeTitle: The Little Carthaginian. Pseudolus. The Rope
label: The Rope (Section 4.8.1268c - Section 5.2.1312)

sections.label: Section 4.8.1268c
sections.translation: And is she to marry me ?

sections.label: Section 4.8.1268d
sections.translation: So I suspect .

sections.label: Section 4.8.1269
sections.original: Censen hodie despondebit eam mihi , quaéso ?
sections.translation: Prithee , do you reckon that he will betroth her to me ?

sections.label: Section 4.8.1269b
sections.translation: So I reckon [*] .

sections.label: Section 4.8.1270
sections.original: Quid ? patri etiam gratulabor cum illam invenit ?
sections.translation: Well , shall I congratulate her father too upon his finding her ?

sections.label: Section 4.8.1270b
sections.translation: So I reckon .

sections.label: Section 4.8.1271
sections.translation: Well , her mother too ?

sections.label: Section 4.8.1271b
sections.translation: So I reckon .

sections.label: Section 4.8.1271c
sections.translation: What then do you reckon ?

sections.label: Section 4.8.1271d
sections.translation: What you ask me , I reckon .

sections.label: Section 4.8.1272b
sections.translation: Tell me then how much do you reckon it at ?

sections.label: Section 4.8.1272c
sections.translation: What I , I reckon —

sections.label: Section 4.8.1273
sections.original: Adsum equidem , ne censionem semper facias .
sections.translation: Then really , do carry over [*] . Don ’ t be always making a reckoning .

sections.label: Section 4.8.1273b
sections.translation: So I reckon .

sections.label: Section 4.8.1274
sections.translation: What if I run ?

sections.label: Section 4.8.1274b
sections.translation: So I reckon .

sections.label: Section 4.8.1274c
sections.translation: Or rather gently , this way ?

sections.label: Section 4.8.1274d
sections.translation: So I reckon .

sections.label: Section 4.8.1275
sections.translation: Ought I to salute her as well when I arrive ?

sections.label: Section 4.8.1275b
sections.translation: So I reckon .

sections.label: Section 4.8.1275c
sections.translation: Her father too ?

sections.label: Section 4.8.1276
sections.translation: So I reckon .

sections.label: Section 4.8.1276b
sections.translation: After that , her mother ?

sections.label: Section 4.8.1276c
sections.translation: So I reckon .

sections.label: Section 4.8.1276d
sections.translation: And what after that ? When I arrive , should I also embrace her father ?

sections.label: Section 4.8.1277b
sections.translation: So I don ’ t reckon .

sections.label: Section 4.8.1278
sections.translation: Well , her mother ?

sections.label: Section 4.8.1278b
sections.translation: So I don ’ t reckon .

sections.label: Section 4.8.1278c
sections.translation: Well , her own self ?

sections.label: Section 4.8.1278d
sections.translation: So I don ’ t reckon .

sections.label: Section 4.8.1279
sections.original: Perii , dilectum dimisit . nunc non censet , cum volo .
sections.translation: Confusion , he has closed his reckoning [*] ; now when I wish him , he doesn ’ t reckon .

sections.label: Section 4.8.1280
sections.translation: You are not in your senses ; follow me .

sections.label: Section 4.8.1280b
sections.original: Duc me , mi patrone , quo libet . —
sections.translation: Conduct me , my patron , where you please .

sections.label: Section 5.1.1281
sections.original: Quis me ést mortalis miserior qui vivat alter hodie ,
sections.translation: What other mortal being is there living this day more wretched than myself , whom before the commissioned judges [*] Plesidippus has just now cast ? Palaestra has just been taken from me by award . I ’ m ruined outright . But I do believe that Procurers were procreated for mere sport ;

sections.label: Section 5.1.1285
sections.original: ita omnés mortales , si quid est mali lenoni , gaudent .
sections.translation: so much do all persons make sport if any misfortune befalls a Procurer . Now I ’ ll go look here , in the Temple of Venus , for that other female , that her at least I may take away , the only portion of my property that remains .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1288
sections.original: Numquam edepol hodie ad vesperum Gripum inspicietis vivom ,
sections.translation: By the powers , you shall never this day at nightfall behold Gripus alive , unless the wallet is restored to me .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1289b
sections.translation: I ’ m ready to die ; when I hear mentior made anywhere of a wallet , I ’ m thumped , as it were with a stake , upon the breast .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1291
sections.original: Istic scelestus liber est : ego qui in mari prehendi
sections.translation: That scoundrel is free ; I , the person that held the net in the sea , and drew up the wallet , to him you refuse to give anything .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1293
sections.original: Pro di immortales , suo mihi hic sermone arrexit aures .
sections.translation: O ye immortal Gods ! by his talk this person has made me prick up my ears .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1294
sections.original: Cubitum hercle longis litteris signabo iam usquequaque ,
sections.translation: By my troth , in letters a cubit long , I ’ ll immediately post it up in every quarter ,

sections.label: Section 5.2.1295
sections.original: si quis perdiderit vidulum cum auro atque argento multo ,
sections.translation: If any person has lost a wallet with plenty of gold and silver , let him come to Gripus . You shan ’ t keep it as you are wishing .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1297
sections.original: Meum hercle illic homo vidulum scit quí habet , ut ego opinor .
sections.translation: I ’ faith , this person knows , as I think , who has got the wallet . This person must be accosted by me ; ye Gods , aid me , I do entreat you .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1299
sections.original: Quid me intro revocas ? hoc volo hic ante ostium extergere .
sections.translation: Why are you calling me back in-doors ? ( He rubs away at the spit .) I want to clean this here before the door .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1300
sections.original: nam hoc quidem pol e robigine , non est e ferro factum ,
sections.translation: But surely this , I ’ faith , has been made of rust , and not of iron ; so that the more I rub it , it becomes quite red and more slender . Why surely this spit has been drugged [*] ; it does waste away so in my hands .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1303
sections.translation: Save you , young man .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1303b
sections.original: Di te ament cum inraso capite .
sections.translation: May the Gods prosper you with your shorn pate [*] .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1303c
sections.translation: What ’ s going on ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.1304
sections.translation: A spit being cleaned .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1304b
sections.translation: How do you do ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.1304c
sections.original: Quid tu ? num medicus , quaeso , es ?
sections.translation: What are you ? Prithee , are you a medicant [*] ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.1305
sections.original: Immo edepol una littera plus sum quam medicus .
sections.translation: No , I ’ faith , I am more than a medicant by one letter .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1305b
sections.translation: Then you are a mendicant .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1306b
sections.translation: You ’ ve hit it to a nicety [*] .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1306c
sections.translation: Your appearance seems suitable to it . But what ’ s the matter with you ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.1307b
sections.original: Hac proxima nocte in marí elavi .
sections.translation: Troth , this last night I was shipwrecked at sea the vessel was cast away , and to my misfortune I lost there everything that I had .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1309
sections.translation: What did you lose ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.1309b
sections.original: Vidulum cum auro atque argento multo .
sections.translation: A wallet with plenty of gold and silver .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1310
sections.original: Ecquid meministi , in vidulo qui periit quid ibi infuerit ?
sections.translation: Do you at all remember what there was in the wallet which was lost ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.1311
sections.original: Quid refert , qui periit tamen ? sine hoc , áliud fabulemur .
sections.translation: What matters for us now to be talking of it , if , in spite of it , it ’ s lost ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.1312
sections.original: Quid si ego sciam qui invenerit ? volo ex te scire signa .
sections.translation: What if I know who has found it ? I wish to learn from you the tokens .
